explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by using 'diantz.exe' to compress a file into a CAB archive. It is a direct representation of the rule's detection logic.
This command uses a filename ending in .cab.txt, which may still be processed as a CAB file depending on context. It tests how the rule handles filenames with extensions that imply CAB compression.
This command adds a trailing backslash to the output path. It tests whether the rule is sensitive to path formatting and trailing slashes.
This command adds a verbose flag (-v) to test if the rule is sensitive to additional command-line arguments that do not affect the core behavior of CAB compression.
This command uses an alternative flag format (/v instead of -v) to test if the rule is sensitive to flag syntax variations.
